tidyfault 0.0.0.9
  • Reference

    calculate() Function

    calculate.Rd

    This function takes a supplied function f and computes the system failure outcomes (1 = failure vs. 0 = not) given every possible combination of input events failing (1) or not failing (0). Outputs a data.frame.

    calculate(f)

    Arguments

    f

    (Required) Function outputted by formulate(), including arguments for all input events in the fault tree (excluding gates or top events). The function should accept binary (0/1) arguments for each basic event and return a numeric value.

    Value

    A data.frame containing a truth table with:

    • One column for each input event (named according to the function's formal arguments)

    • An outcome column indicating system failure (1) or no failure (0)

    Rows are arranged in descending order by outcome, with failure cases listed first. The truth table contains 2^n rows where n is the number of input events, representing all possible combinations of event states.

    Details

    This function generates a complete truth table by:

    • Extracting the formal arguments from the supplied function to identify all input events

    • Creating a grid of all possible binary combinations (0/1) for each event using tidyr::expand_grid()

    • Evaluating the function for each combination to determine system failure

    • Converting outcomes to binary: any value >= 1 indicates failure (1), otherwise no failure (0)

    The function is designed to work with boolean functions created by formulate() that represent fault tree logic. The outcome threshold (>= 1) allows for functions that may return counts or probabilities rather than strict boolean values.

    See also

    formulate for creating the function from a boolean equation

    Examples

    
# Load dependencies
library(tidyverse)
#> ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
#> ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.2.0
#> ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.5.2
#> ✔ ggplot2   4.0.1     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
#> ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
#> ✔ purrr     1.1.0     
#> ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
#> ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
#> ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
#> ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(tidyfault)

# Load example data into our environment
data("fakenodes")
data("fakeedges")

# Build fault tree function and generate full truth table
formula <- curate(nodes = fakenodes, edges = fakeedges) %>%
   equate() %>%
   formulate()
truth_table <- calculate(formula)
truth_table
#> # A tibble: 16 × 5
#>        A     B     C     D outcome
#>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>
#>  1     0     1     1     0       1
#>  2     0     1     1     1       1
#>  3     1     1     0     1       1
#>  4     1     1     1     0       1
#>  5     1     1     1     1       1
#>  6     0     0     0     0       0
#>  7     0     0     0     1       0
#>  8     0     0     1     0       0
#>  9     0     0     1     1       0
#> 10     0     1     0     0       0
#> 11     0     1     0     1       0
#> 12     1     0     0     0       0
#> 13     1     0     0     1       0
#> 14     1     0     1     0       0
#> 15     1     0     1     1       0
#> 16     1     1     0     0       0

# Optional downstream use with MOCUS minimization
curate(nodes = fakenodes, edges = fakeedges) %>%
   concentrate(method = "mocus_rcpp")
#> [1] "B*C"   "A*B*D"
